query: The discovery digests for the Meridian underwriting engagement are finalized in discovery/digests/. Synthesize them into the skill catalog and stage it on the dashboard so Dana can approve everything before any build starts.

ok digests are done for the acme-finance engagement. what skills should we actually build for this team? i want the full picture — commands, agents, integrations, the works — before anything gets written

dana looked at the Plan tab and left notes — merge the three deck skills into one and split sql-runbook by audience. nothing has been built yet, so update catalog.json accordingly and restage it for her

we've got 14 digests from the northwind support team plus engagement.md. figure out which of their workflows should become skills vs thin commands, which need a zendesk integration, and flag anything the digests can't settle
the Plan tab on dashboard.html still shows 9 skills but catalog.json has 11 after my edits — get the halcyon engagement's plan staged correctly and republish the artifact link the champion uses

Before we build anything for the atlas-legal team, I want the proposed plugin surface designed from the digests — success criteria on every skill, and every unsettled judgment call logged to .build/open-questions.json.
digests are in for veritas-claims. draft the catalog, walk me through the judgment calls you made and the counts by phase, then send me the dashboard link so i can approve each item

the copperfield digests mention three report formats and a weekly ops review ritual — should that be one skill or four? make the call, write the catalog entries, and note the reasoning somewhere I can see it

Design the plugin structure for the Brightline onboarding engagement: pick lifecycle phases from their brief, type each skill as document, workflow, or integration, and mark the vendor skills we shouldn't build ourselves.

add two skills to the finch-analytics plan — the SME interview surfaced a contract-redline workflow we completely missed. revise catalog.json without regenerating the whole thing, and keep the existing status fields intact
here's the sharepoint export from the meridian team — playbooks, templates, and two recorded interviews. turn all of it into digests so we can plan the plugin next week
the acme-finance catalog was approved yesterday. build the pilot subset — the three intake skills first — and run the verify loop on each one

dana exported her review notes from the dashboard about the built skills — the deck skill bundles the wrong template and two descriptions overlap. apply her feedback and re-verify everything she flagged
run the trigger benchmark on the northwind plugin and tell me whether the catalog's descriptions hit the hold-out accuracy target, then iterate on the misses
how should i write the description for the sql-runbook skill so it wins triggers against the deck skill? what does the doctrine say about siblings competing for the same queries
evals passed for veritas-claims — package the plugin into an installable marketplace, stand up the team site with the install guide, and put together the rollout plan for the workshop
help me plan the sprint for our platform team — we need to sequence the billing migration and the on-call rotation changes across Q3 and figure out who owns what
interview the SME at brightline about their claims escalation workflow — we still have open questions from the last session that need answers before the plan can be committed
draft a catalog of every API endpoint in our billing service with the owning team for each — markdown table is fine, group by service area
we built the whole ostara plugin last week and my champion says the phase names feel wrong and two of the skills should be merged — reshape the catalog to match her notes and rerun verification
